selection_reasons.repair-falsify-5x3-efficient: pooled champion across 22 fresh panels: 123/288 exact; 39.6% weakest family

source: mechanical anchors plus iteration 25 Sol director

strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: Retain the pooled champion unchanged. Its parallel falsifying repair panel remains the principal replicated control for answer-generating review.
strategies.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Replicate the exact full-bank candidate-only selector. Across Iterations 24 and 25 it has positive correction evidence, while another fresh panel is needed to determine whether its three-selector conversion advantage over repair is stable.
strategies.name: Nine-Bank Falsifying Selection Panel

strategies.hypothesis: Replicate the exact efficiency ablation after its Iteration 25 exact tie with three selectors. If the tie persists without a meaningful weakest-family or partial-accuracy loss, two selector calls per case can be removed.
strategies.name: Nine-Bank One-Selector Falsifying Selection

strategies.hypothesis: Retain the frozen orthogonal pipeline unchanged. Its recurring coverage gains remain valuable evidence about candidate generation, while replication must measure whether the recent harmful integration rate persists.
strategies.name: Blind Regeneration then Falsifying Integration
